narrative: OMX30 opens in a difficult spot: European peer weakness and a sharp US 10Y yield move (+0.88%) are conflicting headwinds despite positive Asia overnight and a 6.0% bull-market cushion above the 200-MA. The ADX at 0.7 is dangerously weak with bearish directional bias, and Stochastic K at 10.5 signals oversold conditions—both reduce confidence in directional calls to 30% magnitude discount. MACD already below signal, %B in lower half, and RSI neutral at 50.5 suggest the market is digesting a momentum plateau. USD/SEK weakness (-0.49%) dulls the exporter narrative (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Ericsson), while rising rates hurt real estate refinancing. Oil's +1.52% gains create cross-currents: positive for Boliden on copper+gold, but negative for Industrials and Consumer on input/fuel costs.
morningBrief: Expect a cautious open into minor red on correlation contagion from Europe and yield volatility. ADX reading of 0.7 is critically weak—treat all technical signals as low-conviction; reduce position sizing. Banks see a mild positive from the 10Y yield spike, but real estate and tech/telecom face headwinds. Industrials face margin pressure from +1.52% oil. Boliden and mining are the single bright spot (copper +0.18%, gold steady), but watch if oil's rally spills into broader energy/input cost fears. Key watch: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—any print below 400k could rekindle yield rally and pressure defensives. Support at 3085 (lower Bollinger); resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Until ADX moves above 18, treat any moves as range-bound noise.
drivers: European weakness (DAX -0.99%, CAC -0.46%) sets negative regional tone
US 10Y yield spike (+0.88%) weighs on tech/telecom valuations despite slight SEK strength
Weak ADX (0.7) + Stochastic oversold (K=10.5) = low conviction, reduced signal reliability
Oil +1.52% pressures industrials and consumer spending despite commodity tailwind to miners

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +0.88% to 4.609% widens NIM expectations; however, weak ADX reduces conviction magnitude by 30%.
Rising US yields signal tighter global financing conditions; refinancing costs rise for leveraged property portfolios.
NASDAQ -1.55% overnight, US yield spike compresses DCF valuations. USD/SEK weakness (-0.49%) removes exporter tailwind.
Brent oil +1.52% raises input costs; USD/SEK weakness (-0.49%) undermines export pricing. Partially offset by bull-market positioning (+6.0% vs 200-MA).
Copper +0.18%, gold stable. Copper gains and steady precious metals support Boliden revenue; SSAB benefits from industrial demand resilience despite oil headwinds.
Oil +1.52% and consumer spending pressure; EUR/SEK weakness may raise H&M sourcing costs. Risk-off tone dampens discretionary appetite.

watchList.reason: Copper +0.18% and gold flat provide direct revenue tailwind; only real outperformer theme in weak macro backdrop.
NASDAQ -1.55% overnight + US yield spike compress tech DCF. USD/SEK weakness removes exporter support.
Yield spike supports NIM, but watch for contagion from European bank weakness (DAX -0.99%).
EUR/SEK weakness (-0.15%), oil up, consumer headwinds. Risk-off environment hits discretionary retail.
Real estate structural headwind from rising yields; property refinancing cycle tightens.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) is primary risk event today. Print below 400k could reignite yield rally and pressure defensive rotations. No major Swedish calendar releases.
topRisk: US yield volatility spillover + European contagion if equity weakness accelerates. Trade war rhetoric (Trump/China headlines) could spike VIX from 16.34 and trigger risk-off cascade. Weak ADX makes whipsaws likely.
